content.text: I'll read the pinned items lock file and verify the manifest.

input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json, collections
d = json.load(open('results/experiments/showcase-fanout/2026-06-12-F-04/items/items.lock.json'))
items = d['items']
print("item_count field:", d['item_count'], "| actual:", len(items))
ids = [i['id'] for i in items]
print("ids sequential:", ids == [f"item-{n:02d}" for n in range(1,33)])
paths = [i['path'] for i in items]
dups = [p for p,c in collections.Counter(paths).items() if c>1]
print("dup paths:", dups or "none")
shas = [i['file_sha256'] for i in items]
print("dup file shas:", len(shas)-len(set(shas)) or "none")
print("pinned_sha:", d['pinned_sha'])
print("generator_git_sha:", d['generator_git_sha'])
print("schema:", d['schema'])
toks = [i['est_prompt_tokens'] for i in items]
print(f"est_prompt_tokens min/max/total: {min(toks)}/{max(toks)}/{sum(toks)}")
lines = [i['line_count'] for i in items]
print(f"line_count min/max: {min(lines)}/{max(lines)}")
tgts = sum(len(i['targets']) for i in items)
print("total targets:", tgts)
empty = [i['id'] for i in items if not i['targets']]
print("items with no targets:", empty or "none")
EOF

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Run arm F: two Claude supervisor calls plus the local 8-stream fan-out dispatcher.
3	set -euo pipefail
4	
5	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
6	cd "$ROOT"
7	
8	ARM="${ARM:-F}"
9	REP="${REP:-01}"
10	RUN_ID="${RUN_ID:-$(date +%F)-${ARM}-${REP}}"
11	RUN_DIR="${RUN_DIR:-$ROOT/results/experiments/showcase-fanout/$RUN_ID}"
12	CORPUS="${CORPUS:-$ROOT/.worktrees/showcase/$RUN_ID/scrapy}"
13	ITEMS_DIR="$RUN_DIR/items"
14	MODEL="${MODEL:-C18-qwen3-30b-vllm}"
15	BASE_URL="${BASE_URL:-http://127.0.0.1:8091/v1}"
16	PORT="${PORT:-8091}"
17	IMAGE="${VLLM_IMAGE:-vllm/vllm-openai:v0.22.1}"
18	CONTAINER="${VLLM_CONTAINER:-fanout-$RUN_ID}"
19	HF_CACHE="${HF_CACHE:-$HOME/.cache/huggingface}"
20	STARTED_CONTAINER=0
21	LOG_PID=""
22	TELEMETRY_PID=""
23	
24	cleanup() {
25	  if [ -n "${TELEMETRY_PID:-}" ] && kill -0 "$TELEMETRY_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
26	    kill "$TELEMETRY_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
27	    wait "$TELEMETRY_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
28	  fi
29	  if [ "$STARTED_CONTAINER" -eq 1 ]; then
30	    docker stop "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
31	  fi
32	  if [ -n "${LOG_PID:-}" ]; then
33	    wait "$LOG_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
34	  fi
35	}
36	trap cleanup EXIT
37	
38	mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR"/{items,workers,logs,prompts}
39	mkdir -p "$(dirname "$CORPUS")"
40	
41	if [ ! -d "$CORPUS/.git" ]; then
42	  git clone https://github.com/scrapy/scrapy.git "$CORPUS"
43	fi
44	git -C "$CORPUS" fetch origin a8ffdcf8517a8973391a14635234b6993b15a86a
45	git -C "$CORPUS" checkout --detach a8ffdcf8517a8973391a14635234b6993b15a86a
46	git -C "$CORPUS" rev-parse HEAD > "$RUN_DIR/corpus_head.txt"
47	
48	python3 scripts/fanout/make_items.py --corpus "$CORPUS" --count 32 --out "$ITEMS_DIR"
49	
50	if docker ps -a --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -Fxq "$CONTAINER"; then
51	  echo "FATAL: container $CONTAINER already exists; not stopping containers this runner did not start" >&2
52	  exit 2
53	fi
54	
55	FLAGS="$(python3 - <<'PY'
56	import json
57	c=json.load(open("bench/configs.json"))
58	print(c["C18-qwen3-30b-vllm"])
59	PY
60	)"
61	SERVED_MODEL="$(python3 - <<'PY'
62	import json, shlex
63	c=json.load(open("bench/configs.json"))
64	a=shlex.split(c["C18-qwen3-30b-vllm"])
65	print(a[a.index("--served-model-name")+1])
66	PY
67	)"
68	
69	docker run -d --rm --gpus all --ipc=host \
70	  -v "$HF_CACHE:/root/.cache/huggingface" \
71	  -p "$PORT:8000" \
72	  --name "$CONTAINER" \
73	  "$IMAGE" $FLAGS > "$RUN_DIR/logs/vllm.container.id"
74	STARTED_CONTAINER=1
75	docker logs -f "$CONTAINER" > "$RUN_DIR/logs/vllm.log" 2>&1 &
76	LOG_PID=$!
77	
78	python3 - <<'PY'
79	import sys
80	sys.path.insert(0, "bench")
81	from common import wait_healthy
82	wait_healthy(base="http://127.0.0.1:8091", tries=360)
83	PY
84	
85	python3 - "$BASE_URL" "$SERVED_MODEL" "$RUN_DIR" <<'PY'
86	import json, pathlib, sys, urllib.request
87	base, model, run_dir = sys.argv[1:]
88	payload = {"model": model, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Say hello."}], "temperature": 0, "max_tokens": 128}
89	req = urllib.request.Request(base.rstrip("/") + "/chat/completions", data=json.dumps(payload).encode(), headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"})
90	with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=120) as r:
91	    body = json.loads(r.read())
92	pathlib.Path(run_dir, "logs", "warmup.json").write_text(json.dumps(body, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
93	PY
94	
95	python3 scripts/telemetry_sampler.py --out "$RUN_DIR/telemetry.csv" &
96	TELEMETRY_PID=$!
97	echo "$TELEMETRY_PID" > "$RUN_DIR/.telemetry.pid"
98	date -Is > "$RUN_DIR/started_at.txt"
99	
100	python3 - "$RUN_DIR" "$RUN_ID" "$IMAGE" "$SERVED_MODEL" "$FLAGS" <<'PY'
101	import json, pathlib, sys
102	run_dir, run_id, image, served_model, flags = sys.argv[1:]
103	body = {
104	    "schema": "showcase-fanout.run.v1",
105	    "run_id": run_id,
106	    "arm": "F",
107	    "run_dir": run_dir,
108	    "corpus": "scrapy",
109	    "local_server": {
110	        "kind": "vllm-openai",
111	        "image": image,
112	        "model_revision": "9b534e4318b7ebc3c961a839f13eb18b1833f441",
113	        "served_model": served_model,
114	        "flags": flags,
115	        "max_num_seqs": 8,
116	        "base_url": "http://127.0.0.1:8091/v1",
117	    },
118	    "fanout": {"items_total": 32, "concurrency": 8},
119	}
120	pathlib.Path(run_dir, "run.json").write_text(json.dumps(body, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
121	PY
122	
123	cat > "$RUN_DIR/prompts/supervisor-decomposition.txt" <<EOF
124	You are the Fable 5 supervisor for the pinned showcase fan-out benchmark.
125	Read $ITEMS_DIR/items.lock.json, confirm there are exactly 32 Scrapy docstring
126	backfill items at the pinned SHA, note any anomalies, and confirm the local
127	dispatcher command the runner will execute next. Do not inspect worker
128	transcripts or do per-item docstring work.
129	EOF
130	
131	claude -p "$(cat "$RUN_DIR/prompts/supervisor-decomposition.txt")" \
132	  --output-format stream-json --verbose \
133	  --mcp-config '{"mcpServers":{}}' --strict-mcp-config > "$RUN_DIR/logs/supervisor-decomposition.jsonl" 2>&1
134	
135	python3 scripts/fanout/dispatch.py \
136	  --run-dir "$RUN_DIR" \
137	  --items-dir "$ITEMS_DIR" \
138	  --corpus "$CORPUS" \
139	  --base-url "$BASE_URL" \
140	  --model "$SERVED_MODEL" \
141	  --concurrency 8
142	
143	python3 scripts/compute_run_metrics.py "$RUN_DIR"
144	
145	cat > "$RUN_DIR/prompts/supervisor-synthesis.txt" <<EOF
146	You are the Fable 5 supervisor for the pinned showcase fan-out benchmark.
147	Read $RUN_DIR/scorecard.json and $RUN_DIR/metrics.json only, then write
148	$RUN_DIR/summary.md with pass rate, retries, wall clock, token totals, and
149	notable instrumentation caveats. Do not inspect individual worker transcripts.
150	EOF
151	
152	claude -p "$(cat "$RUN_DIR/prompts/supervisor-synthesis.txt")" \
153	  --output-format stream-json --verbose \
154	  --mcp-config '{"mcpServers":{}}' --strict-mcp-config > "$RUN_DIR/logs/supervisor-synthesis.jsonl" 2>&1
155	
156	date -Is > "$RUN_DIR/ended_at.txt"
157	echo "$RUN_DIR"
158

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run arm F: two Claude supervisor calls plus the local 8-stream fan-out dispatcher.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

ARM="${ARM:-F}"
REP="${REP:-01}"
RUN_ID="${RUN_ID:-$(date +%F)-${ARM}-${REP}}"
RUN_DIR="${RUN_DIR:-$ROOT/results/experiments/showcase-fanout/$RUN_ID}"
CORPUS="${CORPUS:-$ROOT/.worktrees/showcase/$RUN_ID/scrapy}"
ITEMS_DIR="$RUN_DIR/items"
MODEL="${MODEL:-C18-qwen3-30b-vllm}"
BASE_URL="${BASE_URL:-http://127.0.0.1:8091/v1}"
PORT="${PORT:-8091}"
IMAGE="${VLLM_IMAGE:-vllm/vllm-openai:v0.22.1}"
CONTAINER="${VLLM_CONTAINER:-fanout-$RUN_ID}"
HF_CACHE="${HF_CACHE:-$HOME/.cache/huggingface}"
STARTED_CONTAINER=0
LOG_PID=""
TELEMETRY_PID=""

cleanup() {
  if [ -n "${TELEMETRY_PID:-}" ] && kill -0 "$TELEMETRY_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
    kill "$TELEMETRY_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
    wait "$TELEMETRY_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
  if [ "$STARTED_CONTAINER" -eq 1 ]; then
    docker stop "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  fi
  if [ -n "${LOG_PID:-}" ]; then
    wait "$LOG_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
}
trap cleanup EXIT

mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR"/{items,workers,logs,prompts}
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$CORPUS")"

if [ ! -d "$CORPUS/.git" ]; then
  git clone https://github.com/scrapy/scrapy.git "$CORPUS"
fi
git -C "$CORPUS" fetch origin a8ffdcf8517a8973391a14635234b6993b15a86a
git -C "$CORPUS" checkout --detach a8ffdcf8517a8973391a14635234b6993b15a86a
git -C "$CORPUS" rev-parse HEAD > "$RUN_DIR/corpus_head.txt"

python3 scripts/fanout/make_items.py --corpus "$CORPUS" --count 32 --out "$ITEMS_DIR"

if docker ps -a --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -Fxq "$CONTAINER"; then
  echo "FATAL: container $CONTAINER already exists; not stopping containers this runner did not start" >&2
  exit 2
fi

FLAGS="$(python3 - <<'PY'
import json
c=json.load(open("bench/configs.json"))
print(c["C18-qwen3-30b-vllm"])
PY
)"
SERVED_MODEL="$(python3 - <<'PY'
import json, shlex
c=json.load(open("bench/configs.json"))
a=shlex.split(c["C18-qwen3-30b-vllm"])
print(a[a.index("--served-model-name")+1])
PY
)"

docker run -d --rm --gpus all --ipc=host \
  -v "$HF_CACHE:/root/.cache/huggingface" \
  -p "$PORT:8000" \
  --name "$CONTAINER" \
  "$IMAGE" $FLAGS > "$RUN_DIR/logs/vllm.container.id"
STARTED_CONTAINER=1
docker logs -f "$CONTAINER" > "$RUN_DIR/logs/vllm.log" 2>&1 &
LOG_PID=$!

python3 - <<'PY'
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "bench")
from common import wait_healthy
wait_healthy(base="http://127.0.0.1:8091", tries=360)
PY

python3 - "$BASE_URL" "$SERVED_MODEL" "$RUN_DIR" <<'PY'
import json, pathlib, sys, urllib.request
base, model, run_dir = sys.argv[1:]
payload = {"model": model, "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Say hello."}], "temperature": 0, "max_tokens": 128}
req = urllib.request.Request(base.rstrip("/") + "/chat/completions", data=json.dumps(payload).encode(), headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=120) as r:
    body = json.loads(r.read())
pathlib.Path(run_dir, "logs", "warmup.json").write_text(json.dumps(body, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
PY

python3 scripts/telemetry_sampler.py --out "$RUN_DIR/telemetry.csv" &
TELEMETRY_PID=$!
echo "$TELEMETRY_PID" > "$RUN_DIR/.telemetry.pid"
date -Is > "$RUN_DIR/started_at.txt"

python3 - "$RUN_DIR" "$RUN_ID" "$IMAGE" "$SERVED_MODEL" "$FLAGS" <<'PY'
import json, pathlib, sys
run_dir, run_id, image, served_model, flags = sys.argv[1:]
body = {
    "schema": "showcase-fanout.run.v1",
    "run_id": run_id,
    "arm": "F",
    "run_dir": run_dir,
    "corpus": "scrapy",
    "local_server": {
        "kind": "vllm-openai",
        "image": image,
        "model_revision": "9b534e4318b7ebc3c961a839f13eb18b1833f441",
        "served_model": served_model,
        "flags": flags,
        "max_num_seqs": 8,
        "base_url": "http://127.0.0.1:8091/v1",
    },
    "fanout": {"items_total": 32, "concurrency": 8},
}
pathlib.Path(run_dir, "run.json").write_text(json.dumps(body, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
PY

cat > "$RUN_DIR/prompts/supervisor-decomposition.txt" <<EOF
You are the Fable 5 supervisor for the pinned showcase fan-out benchmark.
Read $ITEMS_DIR/items.lock.json, confirm there are exactly 32 Scrapy docstring
backfill items at the pinned SHA, note any anomalies, and confirm the local
dispatcher command the runner will execute next. Do not inspect worker
transcripts or do per-item docstring work.
EOF

claude -p "$(cat "$RUN_DIR/prompts/supervisor-decomposition.txt")" \
  --output-format stream-json --verbose \
  --mcp-config '{"mcpServers":{}}' --strict-mcp-config > "$RUN_DIR/logs/supervisor-decomposition.jsonl" 2>&1

python3 scripts/fanout/dispatch.py \
  --run-dir "$RUN_DIR" \
  --items-dir "$ITEMS_DIR" \
  --corpus "$CORPUS" \
  --base-url "$BASE_URL" \
  --model "$SERVED_MODEL" \
  --concurrency 8

python3 scripts/compute_run_metrics.py "$RUN_DIR"

cat > "$RUN_DIR/prompts/supervisor-synthesis.txt" <<EOF
You are the Fable 5 supervisor for the pinned showcase fan-out benchmark.
Read $RUN_DIR/scorecard.json and $RUN_DIR/metrics.json only, then write
$RUN_DIR/summary.md with pass rate, retries, wall clock, token totals, and
notable instrumentation caveats. Do not inspect individual worker transcripts.
EOF

claude -p "$(cat "$RUN_DIR/prompts/supervisor-synthesis.txt")" \
  --output-format stream-json --verbose \
  --mcp-config '{"mcpServers":{}}' --strict-mcp-config > "$RUN_DIR/logs/supervisor-synthesis.jsonl" 2>&1

date -Is > "$RUN_DIR/ended_at.txt"
echo "$RUN_DIR"

Verification complete. Here is the supervisor confirmation for run `2026-06-12-F-04`.

## Manifest check: PASS — exactly 32 items at the pinned SHA

From `items/items.lock.json` (schema `showcase-fanout.items.v1`):

- **Item count:** `item_count` field is 32 and the `items` array actually contains 32 entries, with IDs sequential `item-01` … `item-32` and no gaps. All 32 per-item JSON files are present alongside the lock file.
- **Pinned SHA:** `pinned_sha` is `a8ffdcf8517a8973391a14635234b6993b15a86a` (scrapy/scrapy.git), and `corpus_head.txt` in the run dir records the same SHA — the checked-out corpus matches the lock.
- **Integrity:** zero duplicate file paths, zero duplicate `file_sha256` values, and no item has an empty `targets` list. All items are Scrapy source files (commands, extensions, downloader core, http, pipelines, utils), consistent with docstring backfill.
- **Generator provenance:** `generator_git_sha` is `7131297233…`, which matches this repo's current HEAD commit (`7131297 fix(fanout): prompt states each target's required parameter names`) — the items were generated by the current tooling.

## Anomalies

Nothing blocking. Two observations worth recording:

1. **Uneven per-item load:** target counts range from 26 (item-01, `scrapy/http/cookies.py`) down to 6 (e.g. item-32), and `est_prompt_tokens` spans 1288–4412 (total ~75k). All items fit comfortably within the server's 16384 `max-model-len`, so this is a load-balance note, not a risk.
2. **Run state is as expected for this phase:** `workers/` is empty and only the decomposition prompt exists — the fan-out has not started yet, which is correct since this supervisor call runs immediately before dispatch.

## Dispatcher command confirmed

Per `scripts/fanout/run_fleet_arm.sh:135-141`, the runner executes next (with this run's variables resolved):

```
python3 scripts/fanout/dispatch.py \
  --run-dir  results/experiments/showcase-fanout/2026-06-12-F-04 \
  --items-dir results/experiments/showcase-fanout/2026-06-12-F-04/items \
  --corpus   .worktrees/showcase/2026-06-12-F-04/scrapy \
  --base-url http://127.0.0.1:8091/v1 \
  --model    C18-qwen3-30b-vllm \
  --concurrency 8
```

This matches `run.json` exactly: served model `C18-qwen3-30b-vllm` (vLLM `v0.22.1`, revision `9b534e43…`, `--max-num-seqs 8`) at `http://127.0.0.1:8091/v1`, fanning out 32 items at concurrency 8. The warmup completion (`logs/warmup.json`) and telemetry sampler are already in place. Clear to dispatch.

tool_input.command:
python3 - <<'EOF'
import json, collections
d = json.load(open('results/experiments/showcase-fanout/2026-06-12-F-04/items/items.lock.json'))
items = d['items']
print("item_count field:", d['item_count'], "| actual:", len(items))
ids = [i['id'] for i in items]
print("ids sequential:", ids == [f"item-{n:02d}" for n in range(1,33)])
paths = [i['path'] for i in items]
dups = [p for p,c in collections.Counter(paths).items() if c>1]
print("dup paths:", dups or "none")
shas = [i['file_sha256'] for i in items]
print("dup file shas:", len(shas)-len(set(shas)) or "none")
print("pinned_sha:", d['pinned_sha'])
print("generator_git_sha:", d['generator_git_sha'])
print("schema:", d['schema'])
toks = [i['est_prompt_tokens'] for i in items]
print(f"est_prompt_tokens min/max/total: {min(toks)}/{max(toks)}/{sum(toks)}")
lines = [i['line_count'] for i in items]
print(f"line_count min/max: {min(lines)}/{max(lines)}")
tgts = sum(len(i['targets']) for i in items)
print("total targets:", tgts)
empty = [i['id'] for i in items if not i['targets']]
print("items with no targets:", empty or "none")
EOF
